message:
test(sqlite): add regression test for blocked cache rebuild after status changes

Covers beads_rust-ok70. Root cause was fsqlite prepared-statement cache
corruption (fixed in b7eba76); this test exercises status-change →
blocked cache rebuild with complex dep graphs (parent-child + blocking
edges) to prevent recurrence.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"beads_rust-od2j","title":"Unit tests: Entry point modules (main.rs, lib.rs, logging.rs)","description":"# Unit Tests for Entry Point Modules

## Background
These core modules lack unit tests and are only tested indirectly via E2E.

## Modules to Test

### main.rs
- CLI argument parsing
- Command dispatch logic
- Error code mapping
- Global flag handling (--json, --verbose, --quiet)

### lib.rs
- Public API surface
- Module re-exports
- Feature flag conditionals

### logging.rs
- Logger initialization (init_logging)
- Test logger (init_test_logging)
- Log level parsing
- Tracing subscriber configuration

## Test Cases

### main.rs Tests
1. Parse valid command arguments
2. Parse global flags (--json, --verbose)
3. Error exit codes match expected
4. Version output format
5. Help output contains all commands

### lib.rs Tests
6. Public API accessible
7. Feature-gated modules conditional
8. No panic on initialization

### logging.rs Tests
9. init_logging doesn't panic
10. init_test_logging is idempotent
11. Log level respects RUST_LOG env
12. Test writer captures output

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 3 modules have #[cfg(test)] sections
- [ ] 12+ unit test functions total
- [ ] No mocks - test real behavior
- [ ] Tests pass in isolation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"JadeBeaver","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T14:28:09.580033086Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T15:18:47.534259133Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T15:18:47.534259133Z","close_reason":"Added unit tests for main/lib/logging; ran cargo test --lib and cargo test --bin br","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-od2j","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-an3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-ok3g","title":"Propagate MAX_TITLE_CHARS to all consumer sites across the codebase","description":"SUBTASK 1.6: After MAX_TITLE_CHARS is defined in validation/mod.rs, update every other location that hardcodes 500 for the title limit.

CONSUMER SITES (exhaustive list):

1. src/mcp/tools.rs:553 — CreateIssueTool validation: if title.is_empty() || title.chars().count() > 500
   Change to: use crate::validation::MAX_TITLE_CHARS; ... > MAX_TITLE_CHARS

2. src/mcp/tools.rs:1131 — UpdateIssueTool title validation: same pattern
   Change to: > MAX_TITLE_CHARS

3. src/storage/schema.rs:21 — SQL DDL: title TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(length(title) <= 500)
   Change to: format!("title TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(length(title) <= {MAX_TITLE_CHARS})")
   NOTE: This is in a static SQL string (SCHEMA_SQL). If it is a const &str, it cannot use format!(). In that case, add a comment referencing MAX_TITLE_CHARS and note the coupling. Alternatively, if the schema is built dynamically, use the const.

4. src/storage/schema.rs:747 — Migration SQL: "TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(length(title) <= 500)"
   Same approach as #3.

5. src/storage/sqlite.rs:10441 — Another SQL DDL occurrence
   Same approach as #3.

6. MCP tool description strings at tools.rs lines ~828, ~1070, ~1081, ~1314 — these mention "max 500 characters" in the tool schema descriptions that agents read. Update the description strings to reference the actual limit value.

IMPORTANT: For SQL DDL strings that are compile-time constants (const &str), you cannot use format!() at const time in stable Rust. The approach is: (a) if the string is built at runtime (in a function), use format!(), or (b) if it is a top-level const, add a comment "// Must match validation::MAX_TITLE_CHARS" and leave the literal, or (c) use the const_format crate. Evaluate which approach fits best given the existing code structure.

DEPENDS ON: Subtask 1.1 (defining the const).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:43.414588914Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:54:37.225662655Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:54:37.225227488Z","close_reason":"Propagated MAX_TITLE_CHARS to mcp/tools.rs (validation logic + coupling comments in schema descriptions), schema.rs (coupling comment), sqlite.rs (coupling comment). SQL DDL strings use comments since they cannot interpolate Rust consts.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cross-cutting"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-ok3g","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-9oiv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:51.485486794Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":240,"issue_id":"beads_rust-ok3g","author":"ubuntu","text":"CRITICAL CONSIDERATION: SQL DDL strings in schema.rs and sqlite.rs may be compile-time const &str. Rust does not support format!() in const context. Options: (1) If the DDL is built in a function at runtime, use format!() with the const. (2) If it is a top-level const &str, add a comment // Must match validation::MAX_TITLE_CHARS = 500 and leave the literal. (3) Use the const_format crate for compile-time format strings. Option 2 is simplest and least invasive. The coupling comment ensures future changes are caught in review.","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:21Z"}]}
-{"id":"beads_rust-ok70","title":"Fix blocked cache rebuild SQL parse error after issue writes","description":"Observed in the repo-local binary after verifying and closing beads_rust-586v. `./target/debug/br update beads_rust-586v --status in_progress --json` fails with `Database error: SQL error at offset 13: unexpected token at start of statement`. `./target/debug/br close beads_rust-586v ... --json` successfully persists the close, then fails while rebuilding the blocked cache with the same SQL parse error. Ready/show/doctor continue to work. This likely lives in the write-side cache rebuild / post-mutation path rather than markdown import itself.

Repro:
1. `./target/debug/br update beads_rust-586v --status in_progress --json`
2. `./target/debug/br close beads_rust-586v --reason ... --json`

Acceptance:
- update/close succeed without SQL parse errors
- blocked cache rebuild completes successfully after writes
- regression coverage exercises the current failure path","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:13:34.527928302Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:17:51.143865776Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Ready/show/doctor continue to work. This likely lives in the write-side cache rebuild / post-mutation path rather than markdown import itself.

Repro:
1. `./target/debug/br update beads_rust-586v --status in_progress --json`
2. `./target/debug/br close beads_rust-586v --reason ... --json`

Acceptance:

Added regression test test_update_status_triggers_successful_cache_rebuild exercising status-change → blocked cache rebuild with complex dep graphs to prevent recurrence.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-opl7","title":"Optimize graph command performance","description":"Optimize calculate_depths in graph command to use O(V+E) instead of O(N^2).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BronzeHawk","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:45.864396226Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:55.270977725Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:55.270904948Z","close_reason":"Optimized calculate_depths to O(V+E)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-oqa","title":"External dependency resolution (external:<project>:<capability>)","description":"# External Dependency Resolution (external:<project>:<capability>)

## Purpose
Support classic external dependency semantics for ready/blocked and dependency trees.

## Encoding
- External dependency ID: `external:<project>:<capability>`
- Satisfied when external project has a **closed** issue labeled `provides:<capability>`.

## Config
- `external_projects` in config.yaml maps project name → path.

## Behavior
- External deps are **not** stored in blocked cache.
- Evaluated at query time (ready/blocked/tree) to avoid multi-DB work during cache rebuild.
- Open each external project DB **once per project**, batch by capability.

## Output
- In dep tree, synthesize external leaf nodes:
  - status `closed` if satisfied, else `blocked`
  - title prefixed `✓` or `⏳`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Ready/blocked filters respect external deps.
- External deps appear in dep tree (down direction only).

## Tests
- Mock external project with provides labels.
- Unresolved external deps remain blocking.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:03:46.203704905Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T03:53:40.739867167Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T03:53:40.739867167Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-oqw","title":"Shell Completions","description":"## Overview
Implement shell completion scripts for bash, zsh, fish, and PowerShell using clap_complete. This enables tab-completion for commands, options, and issue IDs.

## Technical Requirements

### Clap Complete Integration
```rust
use clap_complete::{generate, Generator, Shell};

fn generate_completions<G: Generator>(gen: G, cmd: &mut Command, name: &str, out: &mut dyn Write) {
    generate(gen, cmd, name, out);
}

// CLI command to generate completions
#[derive(Subcommand)]
enum CompletionCommands {
    /// Generate shell completions
    Completions {
        #[arg(value_enum)]
        shell: Shell,
    },
}

fn handle_completions(shell: Shell) {
    let mut cmd = Cli::command();
    match shell {
        Shell::Bash => generate_completions(Shell::Bash, &mut cmd, "br", &mut io::stdout()),
        Shell::Zsh => generate_completions(Shell::Zsh, &mut cmd, "br", &mut io::stdout()),
        Shell::Fish => generate_completions(Shell::Fish, &mut cmd, "br", &mut io::stdout()),
        Shell::PowerShell => generate_completions(Shell::PowerShell, &mut cmd, "br", &mut io::stdout()),
        _ => eprintln!("Unsupported shell"),
    }
}
```

### Dynamic Completions
For dynamic values like issue IDs:
```rust
// Custom completer for issue IDs (zsh/fish support)
fn complete_issue_ids() -> Vec<String> {
    // Read from cache file or query DB
    if let Ok(contents) = fs::read_to_string(".beads/.id_cache") {
        contents.lines().map(|s| s.to_string()).collect()
    } else {
        Vec::new()
    }
}
```

### Installation Instructions
```
# Bash (add to ~/.bashrc)
eval "$(br completions bash)"

# Zsh (add to ~/.zshrc)
eval "$(br completions zsh)"

# Fish (add to ~/.config/fish/config.fish)
br completions fish | source

# PowerShell (add to $PROFILE)
br completions powershell | Out-String | Invoke-Expression
```

## Completions Provided

### Command Completions
```
br <TAB>
create  update  close  reopen  list  show  ready  blocked
dep     label   comment search  stats sync  config  doctor  prime
```

### Option Completions
```
br create --<TAB>
--title  --type  --priority  --assignee  --labels  --deps
--description  --parent  --due  --json  --robot

br list --<TAB>
--status  --type  --priority  --assignee  --labels
--sort  --limit  --json
```

### Value Completions
```
br create --type=<TAB>
bug  feature  task  epic  question  docs  chore

br list --status=<TAB>
open  in_progress  closed  all

br show <TAB>
beads_rust-abc123  beads_rust-def456  beads_rust-ghi789
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Generate bash completions
- [ ] Generate zsh completions
- [ ] Generate fish completions
- [ ] Generate PowerShell completions
- [ ] Complete command names
- [ ] Complete option names
- [ ] Complete option values (enum types)
- [ ] Dynamic issue ID completion (if feasible)
- [ ] Installation instructions in --help

## Dependencies
- Requires `clap_complete` crate (already in Cargo.toml)
- Requires CLI Skeleton (Command definition)

## Rationale
Shell completions dramatically improve CLI usability. Users can discover available commands and options via tab completion. Dynamic issue ID completion saves typing and reduces errors.
","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T06:34:24.301758676Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T22:56:03.667740156Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T22:56:03.667740156Z","close_reason":"Shell completions fully implemented: bash, zsh, fish, PowerShell, and elvish supported. Command works via 'br completions <shell>'. Installation instructions included. Tests verify completion generation.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}

 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.3","title":"Define the incident bundle capture workflow for field failures","description":"## Purpose
Define the minimal reproducibility bundle we should gather the first time a field failure is reported so we stop losing crucial evidence.

## Bundle Contents
- failing command + stdout/stderr
- `br doctor --json`
- `metadata.json`
- `issues.jsonl`
- presence and hashes of `beads.db`, `beads.db-wal`, `beads.db-shm`, `beads.db-journal`
- environment overrides that affect discovery/path/output
- whether multiple agents/processes were active

## Acceptance Criteria
- The bundle is small enough to request quickly during incident response.
- The captured data is sufficient to classify the failure using the taxonomy from this track.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:25.615887382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T06:23:54.847666702Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T06:23:54.834341839Z","close_reason":"Defined the minimal incident evidence bundle for field failures in docs/ARCHITECTURE.md.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["incident-response","ops","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:25.615887382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:53:55.594416915Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":191,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This bead exists because ad hoc bug reports waste time and collapse into speculation. If we cannot preserve the evidence bundle, we cannot expect to reproduce or eliminate field failures systematically.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:26Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","title":"Build a sanitized real-world workspace failure corpus and replay harness","description":"## Why
Hermetic tempdir tests are not enough. We need a standing corpus of realistic damaged or drifted workspaces and a way to replay them automatically.

## Scope
Create sanitized fixtures that model the important bad states and wire them into a replay harness that exercises open/read/write/doctor/sync behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The corpus includes the representative classes from the taxonomy.
- The replay harness can be run locally and in CI.
- Failures in the corpus are easy to inspect and update intentionally.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:27.047082628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:41.863684637Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fixtures","reliability","replay"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:27.047082628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:41.863085696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":192,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This track converts anecdotal production pain into durable regression assets. The goal is not synthetic benchmark data; it is realistic workspace states that future code must handle safely.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:28Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","title":"Create a sanitized fixture corpus for representative corrupt and drifted workspaces","description":"## Purpose
Build a curated, sanitized fixture corpus for representative workspace failure states.

## Required Fixture Families
- corrupt or partially corrupt `beads.db`
- `beads.db` + stale/missing `-wal`/`-shm` combinations
- malformed schema / duplicate schema-entry states
- JSONL with malformed lines or conflict markers
- DB/JSONL/metadata disagreement
- path/env discovery edge cases that only appear outside hermetic tests
- interrupted rebuild/sync/export leftovers

## Acceptance Criteria
- Fixtures are sanitized and safe to store in-repo.
- Each fixture includes metadata explaining the intended state and expected classification.
- The corpus is organized so future incidents can add new fixtures instead of spawning one-off repro scripts.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:29.035540341Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T12:08:20.419604596Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T12:08:20.414239862Z","close_reason":"Added sanitized workspace-failure fixture corpus and loader support","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["corruption","fixtures","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:04.515270731Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:29.035540341Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":193,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Key consideration: a fixture corpus only helps if it preserves the interesting structure of the failure. Do not over-normalize fixtures into clean states that no longer reproduce the real anomaly.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:30Z"},{"id":220,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Claiming this now. Initial focus: inventory existing malformed/corrupt workspace fixtures and repro directories already in-repo, identify the canonical fixture layout under tests/fixtures or adjacent harness code, then add a sanitized metadata-backed corpus that downstream reliability beads can consume.","created_at":"2026-03-12T11:33:27Z"},{"id":222,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed fixture corpus setup under tests/fixtures/workspace_failures/. Added seven small, metadata-backed workspace cases covering corrupt DB text, JSONL conflict markers, DB/JSONL drift, duplicate config rows, metadata-driven custom paths, WAL-without-SHM sidecars, and interrupted rebuild leftovers. Added loader/support APIs in tests/common/dataset_registry.rs so tests can enumerate fixtures and materialize a transport-safe visible beads/ payload back into runtime .beads/ workspaces. Verification: cargo fmt --check; rch exec -- cargo test workspace_failure_fixture -- --nocapture; rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets; rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings.","created_at":"2026-03-12T12:07:33Z"}]}

Add a harness that mounts each fixture workspace and asserts the expected behavior across the main operational surfaces.

## Required Surfaces
- startup/open
- read commands (`list`, `show`, `ready`, `where`, `info`)
- write commands (`create`, `update`, `close`, `reopen`, `label`, `comments`, `delete` as appropriate)
- `doctor`
- `sync --status`, import/export/rebuild behavior where applicable

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each fixture records expected classification and expected command outcomes.
- The harness distinguishes: healthy, degraded-but-usable, recoverable, and unsafe.
- Regressions are reported in a way that makes the failing fixture/state obvious.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:30.673427289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:22:52.902394074Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T04:22:52.901800343Z","close_reason":"Replay harness verified: fixture manifest expectations, core read surfaces, and core write surfaces pass; cargo check, clippy, and fmt gates passed via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["reliability","replay","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:22.349991073Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:30.673427289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:13.418006391Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":194,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This is the bridge between plan-space and code-space. Once this bead lands, future corruption fixes should start by adding or updating a fixture expectation instead of inventing bespoke local repro steps.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:31Z"},{"id":226,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented explicit fixture command-outcome metadata plus a new tests/workspace_failure_replay.rs harness that replays fresh workspace-failure copies across startup/read/write/doctor/sync surfaces. Verified the manifest contract against the current target/debug/br on all seven fixtures. Cargo verification via rch is currently blocked by a stale worker-side /data/projects/frankensqlite checkout (fsqlite-core remote_effects.rs still calling bulkhead.cancel_entry(entry_id) without the new Time arg), so leaving this bead in_progress until offloaded cargo passes cleanly.","created_at":"2026-03-12T15:52:03Z"}]}
+{"id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","title":"Add a replay harness that asserts command behavior against the fixture corpus","description":"## Purpose
Add a harness that mounts each fixture workspace and asserts the expected behavior across the main operational surfaces.

## Required Surfaces
- startup/open
- read commands (`list`, `show`, `ready`, `where`, `info`)
- write commands (`create`, `update`, `close`, `reopen`, `label`, `comments`, `delete` as appropriate)
- `doctor`
- `sync --status`, import/export/rebuild behavior where applicable

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each fixture records expected classification and expected command outcomes.
- The harness distinguishes: healthy, degraded-but-usable, recoverable, and unsafe.
- Regressions are reported in a way that makes the failing fixture/state obvious.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:30.673427289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:25:40.921032771Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T04:25:40.920735354Z","close_reason":"Replay harness verified: 4 tests pass (manifest expectations, core read, core write, classification coherence), 7 fixtures across 4 classification levels (usable, degraded, recoverable, unsafe), all required surfaces covered.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["reliability","replay","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:22.349991073Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:30.673427289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:13.418006391Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":194,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This is the bridge between plan-space and code-space. Cargo verification via rch is currently blocked by a stale worker-side /data/projects/frankensqlite checkout (fsqlite-core remote_effects.rs still calling bulkhead.cancel_entry(entry_id) without the new Time arg), so leaving this bead in_progress until offloaded cargo passes cleanly.","created_at":"2026-03-12T15:52:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","title":"Generate deterministic long-lived workspace evolution scenarios","description":"## Purpose
Create long-lived workspace scenarios that accumulate real history over time rather than relying only on one-shot fresh temp workspaces.

## Cover
- many commands over one workspace
- restarts between commands
- mixed auto-import/auto-flush behavior
- repeated derived-state rebuilds
- state after partial failures and subsequent recovery attempts

## Acceptance Criteria
- Scenario generator can produce deterministic seeds for reproducible failures.
- Generated workspaces can be fed back into the fixture/replay system.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:32.337089325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T16:00:01.697780433Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T15:59:50.012415288Z","close_reason":"Implemented deterministic long-lived workspace evolution generator and replay handoff","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["long-lived","reliability","state-drift"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:31.233437625Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:32.337089325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:40.459575482Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":195,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Rationale: production users do not reinitialize  for every command. Long-lived state accumulation is where 'suite is green but field is broken' bugs are born.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:33Z"},{"id":224,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Starting now. Plan: add a deterministic long-lived workspace evolution generator in tests/common that can replay many-command histories across restarts, mixed auto-import/auto-flush modes, derived-state rebuilds, and recovery-after-failure paths. I’ll keep the output reusable so generated states can feed back into the fixture/replay corpus.","created_at":"2026-03-12T15:29:36Z"},{"id":227,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented a seeded long-lived workspace evolution layer in tests/common/scenarios.rs. It now supports deterministic multi-command workspace scripts with issue-ID capture, stale/no-auto-import flows, explicit flush/import cycles, JSONL corruption + snapshot restore, and materialization back into DatasetOverride/replay-style inputs. Verification: cargo fmt --check passed; isolated-target cargo check --all-targets passed; workspace_evolution test filter passed locally across the integration binaries that include tests/common. Repo-wide isolated-target clippy now fails only in tests/workspace_failure_replay.rs (parallel bead surface), not in this implementation.","created_at":"2026-03-12T16:00:01Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","title":"Expand crash, concurrency, and filesystem realism coverage","description":"## Why
The current concurrency coverage proves some lock semantics, but it does not yet model real agent swarms, crash timing, filesystem failures, or prolonged mixed usage on one workspace.

## Scope
Expand destructive realism: crash injection, multi-process contention, filesystem anomalies, and long-running stress.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Reliability tests model the kinds of failures users actually trigger accidentally.
- We can answer "what happens if the process dies here?" for the critical write/sync phases.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:34.087986025Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:50.935545188Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["chaos","concurrency","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:34.087986025Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:50.934904899Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":196,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Track intent: move from 'SQLite locking works under a narrow benchmark' to 'the workspace survives hostile real-world timing and storage conditions.'","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:35Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.1","title":"Add a crash-injection matrix for all critical mutation and sync phases","description":"## Purpose
Add crash-injection coverage across the critical mutation and sync phases.

## Required Operations
- create
- update
- close/reopen/defer/undefer
- delete
- label/comment mutation
- sync import/export/rebuild/recovery paths

## Required Crash Points
- before primary write
- after primary write but before derived-state rebuild
- during temp-file write
- before/after rename
- before dirty-flag clear
- during/after backup/quarantine creation

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each crash point has explicit expected post-restart behavior.
- No crash leaves the workspace silently misclassified as healthy.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:35.916711828Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:58.412355354Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crash-consistency","reliability","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:49.444077784Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:58.411766251Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:35.916711828Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":197,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Future-self note: this bead is about interruption semantics, not throughput. Prefer fewer operations with well-defined crash points over broad but shallow coverage.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:37Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","title":"Broaden multi-process and mixed-agent contention scenarios","description":"## Purpose
Expand concurrency coverage from narrow lock-timeout scenarios to realistic multi-process and mixed-agent usage.

## Cover
- concurrent writers on one workspace
- read/write races across commands
- multiple agents issuing different command families interleaved
- routed/external workspace access during local mutation
- background processes touching the same `.beads` directory

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests define which outcomes are acceptable vs unacceptable.
- Contention results in bounded failure/retry behavior, not drift or corruption.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:37.773727464Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T11:38:47.309252200Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T11:31:29.228335520Z","close_reason":"Expanded bounded-failure concurrency coverage and validated integrity invariants","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agents","concurrency","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:09.543615058Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:37.773727464Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":198,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Important consideration: success here does not mean every race magically succeeds. It means failures are explicit, bounded, and non-corrupting.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:39Z"},{"id":218,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Working this now in tests/e2e_concurrency.rs. Scope: add coverage for interleaved command families on one workspace, plus external BEADS_DIR/status access against the same workspace during local mutation, with final integrity assertions to distinguish acceptable lock contention from corruption/drift.","created_at":"2026-03-12T11:07:24Z"},{"id":219,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed in tests/e2e_concurrency.rs. Added env-clearing/env-aware helper coverage, broadened contention assertions to bounded explicit failures vs corruption, added interleaved-command-family and external-BEADS_DIR/background-status scenarios, and tightened existing concurrency cases to assert final integrity based on successful operations. Verification: rch exec -- cargo test --test e2e_concurrency -- --nocapture, rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets, rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check.","created_at":"2026-03-12T11:31:44Z"},{"id":221,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added broader contention coverage in tests/e2e_concurrency.rs for mixed command families, routed/external access, background sync status checks, and actor-oriented mutation scenarios. Also stabilized e2e_concurrent_writes_succeed_with_retry with an explicit lock timeout for deterministic remote verification. Verified with rch exec -- cargo test --test e2e_concurrency -- --nocapture, rch exec -- cargo test --test e2e_concurrency e2e_concurrent_writes_succeed_with_retry -- --nocapture, rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets, and cargo fmt --check.","created_at":"2026-03-12T11:38:47Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","title":"Model filesystem realism and external interference scenarios","description":"## Purpose
Model filesystem and storage anomalies that hermetic tempdir tests almost never hit.

## Cover
- sidecar mismatch and disappearance
- rename failure / interrupted atomic export
- disk-full or temp-file allocation failure
- permissions changes
- external sync/editor/cloud-drive interference on `.beads`
- unusual HOME/path/env interactions that affect discovery and storage location

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests assert data preservation and honest diagnostics.
- We understand which anomalies are tolerated, which trigger repair, and which require operator intervention.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:39.716569260Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:10:43.566934395Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["filesystem","ops","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:19.121325297Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:39.716569260Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":199,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This is where lab conditions diverge sharply from the field. If a scenario only happens on a user's real filesystem, the current tempdir-first suite is likely blind to it.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:41Z"},{"id":228,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Workspace-path audit progress: fixed  redirect provenance, fixed  so missing explicit metadata JSONL no longer falls back to discovered legacy JSONL, fixed  so configured external JSONL paths are validated with the external-path policy instead of being flagged as outside .beads, and strengthened workspace replay assertions to use resolved database/custom metadata paths. Verification is currently limited to rustfmt/git-diff checks and targeted reasoning because the rch worker fleet is unreachable right now and I am avoiding local cargo fallback per repo rules.","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:37:53Z"},{"id":229,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Correction to previous note: fixed where redirect provenance; fixed doctor so a missing explicit metadata JSONL path no longer falls back to a discovered legacy JSONL; fixed doctor so a configured external JSONL path is validated with the external-path policy instead of being flagged as outside .beads; and strengthened workspace replay assertions to use the resolved database/custom metadata paths. Verification is currently limited to rustfmt/git-diff checks and targeted reasoning because the rch worker fleet is unreachable right now and I am avoiding local cargo fallback per repo rules.","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:38:01Z"},{"id":230,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","author":"IcyCitadel","text":"Progress update: audited workspace discovery, where/info/doctor path reporting, and workspace failure replay coverage. Fixed redirect provenance in `where`, fixed `doctor` so a missing explicitly configured metadata JSONL path is surfaced instead of silently falling back to a discovered legacy file, corrected the config unit test for external DB override workspace fallback and added the missing no-workspace error case, and updated workspace replay assertions to validate the resolved database path from `where --json` for custom metadata DB paths. Verification: rustfmt --check passed on touched files; git diff --check passed; rch remote tests passed for discover_beads_dir_with_cli_from_* filters, resolve_where_output_preserves_redirect_origin, and test_collect_doctor_report_surfaces_missing_explicit_metadata_jsonl.","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:49:47Z"},{"id":231,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","author":"IcyCitadel","text":"Follow-up: full rch cargo check --all-targets did not expose new code errors in these touched paths, but it failed remotely on vmi1227854 with No space left on device while compiling unrelated integration-test targets. A narrower rch cargo check --lib still surfaced one local warning in doctor.rs (unused std::fs::self import); I removed that import locally afterward.","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:03:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","title":"Add a long-running single-workspace stress suite","description":"## Purpose
Create a long-running stress suite that reuses one workspace across many commands and processes to surface drift, stale assumptions, and accumulated-state bugs.

## Cover
- repeated issue lifecycle operations
- repeated sync/import/export status checks
- intermittent failures and restarts
- mixed human-style and agent-style command ordering

## Acceptance Criteria
- The suite can run for a meaningful duration without manual babysitting.
- Failures leave behind enough artifacts to classify and reproduce.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:41.731866221Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:35:25.309497352Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T04:35:25.309127179Z","close_reason":"Added deterministic long-lived single-workspace stress suite with mixed create/update/label/comment/close/reopen/sync/read/expected-failure flow; verified targeted stress test, workspace evolution tests, fmt, check, clippy, and ubs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["long-lived","reliability","stress"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:28.600633433Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:41.731866221Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:37.659700575Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":200,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This bead exists because the product is a stateful CLI, not a stateless request handler. Bugs that require dozens or hundreds of operations on the same workspace will never show up in per-test fresh-init patterns.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","title":"Harden detection, recovery, and no-data-loss guarantees","description":"## Why
Even with better tests, the product must behave safely when it encounters a damaged or inconsistent workspace in production. Detection, recovery, and operator messaging need to be consistent and trustworthy.

## Scope
Unify anomaly classification, preserve evidence before repair, verify post-recovery state, and make `doctor`/startup/write behavior agree.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Automatic recovery is explicit, reversible, and validated.
- We do not report "OK" for workspaces that are only partially readable or that fail on writes.
- Recovery logic never silently hides potential data loss.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:43.783459850Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:53:09.162394901Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doctor","recovery","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:43.783459850Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:59.944386891Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:53:09.161785269Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":201,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This track is where field pain becomes product behavior. The product must either operate safely, recover safely, or stop with actionable evidence; the current gray zone in between is unacceptable.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:45Z"}]}
-{"id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","title":"Unify anomalous workspace classification across startup, doctor, and write paths","description":"## Purpose
Create one canonical anomaly classifier that every entry point uses when evaluating a workspace: startup/open, explicit `doctor`, mutation paths, sync, and recovery logic.

## Scope
- define shared anomaly classes for malformed SQLite pages, WAL/SHM mismatch, JSONL parse drift, metadata contradictions, stale dirty/export state, and broken derived caches
- remove ad hoc command-specific interpretations of the same evidence
- ensure structured errors and operator-facing diagnostics use the same language

## Acceptance Criteria
- A given workspace snapshot receives the same classification regardless of which command hits it first.
- Recovery and write decisions are driven by named health states rather than scattered heuristics.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:49:21.619173305Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:21.789181630Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["classification","diagnostics","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:47.622950600Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:57.607408234Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:49:21.619173305Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":203,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This bead is the hinge point for the whole hardening track. If startup says “healthy enough” while writes or sync say “catastrophic corruption,” the user experiences chaos even when each individual code path looks locally reasonable.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:21Z"}]}
+{"id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","title":"Unify anomalous workspace classification across startup, doctor, and write paths","description":"## Purpose
Create one canonical anomaly classifier that every entry point uses when evaluating a workspace: startup/open, explicit `doctor`, mutation paths, sync, and recovery logic.

## Scope
- define shared anomaly classes for malformed SQLite pages, WAL/SHM mismatch, JSONL parse drift, metadata contradictions, stale dirty/export state, and broken derived caches
- remove ad hoc command-specific interpretations of the same evidence
- ensure structured errors and operator-facing diagnostics use the same language

## Acceptance Criteria
- A given workspace snapshot receives the same classification regardless of which command hits it first.
- Recovery and write decisions are driven by named health states rather than scattered heuristics.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:49:21.619173305Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:27:54.249477508Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["classification","diagnostics","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:47.622950600Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:57.607408234Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:49:21.619173305Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":203,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This bead is the hinge point for the whole hardening track. If startup says “healthy enough” while writes or sync say “catastrophic corruption,” the user experiences chaos even when each individual code path looks locally reasonable.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:21Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.2","title":"Quarantine corrupt DB artifacts before automatic rebuild or repair","description":"## Purpose
When corruption or unsafe drift is detected, preserve the original DB artifacts before any rebuild, retry, or repair action mutates evidence.

## Scope
- move or snapshot suspect SQLite artifacts and sidecars into a clearly labeled quarantine area
- record why quarantine happened and what command triggered it
- make rebuild-from-JSONL and similar repair flows operate on fresh targets rather than on top of ambiguous debris

## Acceptance Criteria
- Recovery never silently overwrites the only copy of a damaged DB or WAL.
- Operators can inspect and attach quarantined artifacts to incident reports.
- Automatic repair paths become auditable and reversible.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:50:44.050503419Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:24.853710150Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["quarantine","recovery","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:50:44.050503419Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:56:06.949746141Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":204,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"The root reliability principle here is evidence preservation. A “successful” automatic rebuild that destroys the original failing artifacts is still a product failure because it erases the operator’s ability to diagnose, prove, or safely roll back what happened.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:24Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.3","title":"Verify post-recovery invariants and forbid silent state loss","description":"## Purpose
Every recovery path must end with explicit postconditions proving that user-visible state and internal invariants are acceptable before normal writes continue.

## Scope
- verify issue graph, comments, labels, events, metadata, export markers, sidecars, and derived caches after repair
- detect silent data loss, ID collisions, truncated exports, stale `last_touched`, or self-contradictory counters
- refuse to declare success if postconditions fail

## Acceptance Criteria
- Recovery paths have a machine-checkable “safe to proceed” verdict.
- Silent partial repair is treated as failure, not success.
- Tactical corruption fixes roll into a shared invariant-validation framework.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:08.702874952Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:28.029445713Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["invariants","recovery","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:08.702874952Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:56:16.123909606Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:56:25.768255051Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-v13g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:58:36.942963087Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":205,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"A retry-after-rebuild path is only defensible if the product can prove the rebuilt workspace is coherent. This bead turns “it seems to work now” into explicit post-recovery evidence.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:28Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","title":"Expand doctor to detect drift, sidecar anomalies, and partial-functionality traps","description":"## Purpose
Make `br doctor` the authoritative workspace inspection tool for drift and partial-breakage states, not just an optimistic health check.

## Scope
- detect DB/JSONL divergence, invalid or contradictory metadata, suspicious sidecar leftovers, and partially functioning workspaces where reads pass but writes fail
- expose actionable structured diagnostics and recommended next steps
- ensure doctor classification aligns with startup and mutation decisions

## Acceptance Criteria
- `doctor` can flag the classes of failures that users are actually hitting in the field.
- A workspace that is unsafe for writes is never reported as simply “OK.”","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:51.118030931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:29:52.499800968Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagnostics","doctor","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:56:35.332546461Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:51.118030931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:56:44.594245547Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":206,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"The immediate user complaint is exactly this gap: the system can look healthy from one angle and then fail catastrophically on the next write. `doctor` has to become the honest reporter of those partial-functionality traps.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:31Z"},{"id":232,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","author":"ubuntu","text":"Progress note: patched `br doctor` to add explicit `db.sidecars` error diagnostics and `db.recovery_artifacts` warnings, plus local sidecar quarantine-in-recovery behavior for `doctor --repair`. Added targeted unit tests and a new E2E case for anomalous WAL-without-SHM repair. Claim is still graph-blocked by beads_rust-rsei.4.1 / beads_rust-rsei.4, so this is progress without status transition until verification finishes.","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:29:52Z"}]}
